title: Giannis Antetokounmpo paid high praise to Celtics’ coach Joe Mazzulla
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla, citing his winning mentality despite challenges.
Boston Celtics are second in the Eastern Conference without top scorer Jayson Tatum for most of the season.
Antetokounmpo discussed his frustration with the Bucks' culture under Doc Rivers, contrasting with Boston's approach.

title: Person seriously injured in fall at Firefighting Academy facility
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A person suffered serious injuries after a fall at the Firefighting Academy in Stow, Tuesday morning.
Emergency crews responded and provided medical care; two additional ambulances were needed for assistance.
Weather prevented air transport; the injured person was transported by ground to UMass Memorial Medical Center.

title: TravelMag went on a seafood road trip in Maine and named these ‘can’t-miss’ restaurants. Which is your favorite?
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: TravelMag highlighted 10 'can't-miss' seafood restaurants on a road trip along Maine's coast.
The Clam Shack in Kennebunkport is noted for its legendary fried clams, among other popular stops.
Readers are invited to share their favorite coastal Maine seafood restaurant with Boston.com.

title: Ex-Stoughton cop Matthew Farwell’s DNA was on strap used to strangle Sandra Birchmore, prosecutors say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matthew Farwell's DNA was found on the strap used to strangle Sandra Birchmore in 2021, prosecutors say.
Prosecutors allege Farwell believed he fathered Birchmore's unborn child, but DNA tests excluded him as the father.
Farwell maintains Birchmore's death was a suicide; he is held without bail and has pleaded not guilty.

title: A young mother rescued her 2-year-old son after their car crashed into a Vermont river
author: John Waller
bullets: A 26-year-old mother rescued her 2-year-old son after their car crashed into the Ottauquechee River in Vermont.
Hartford firefighters responded and found the mother holding onto the vehicle as it floated downstream before reaching safety.
The mother and son were evaluated at Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center, with no apparent serious injuries reported.

title: All the places you can go on the Amtrak Downeaster
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Amtrak Downeaster runs a 143-mile route from Boston's North Station to Brunswick, Maine, serving 12 stations.
Passengers enjoy amenities like reclining seats, free Wi-Fi, and refreshments from the Downeaster Cafe, available on board.
One-way fares range from $3 to $38, with discounts for seniors and additional fees for pets and bicycles.

title: ‘Boston College is about to have a renaissance in basketball’: Dan Hurley’s take on Luke Murray
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Luke Murray, 41, is set to lead Boston College basketball after being hired in March.
Dan Hurley praised Murray, noting their deep friendship and predicting a renaissance for BC basketball.
Murray has been Hurley's assistant at UConn since 2021, contributing to two national championships.

title: MBTA sets $80 round-trip train fare for World Cup games
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA sets $80 round-trip fare from South Station to Gillette Stadium for 2026 World Cup matches.
Train tickets are sold exclusively through mTicket app; first five match tickets available April 8.
MBTA to run 14 express trains on match days; other transit services will have extended hours.

title: New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for April 2026
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix releases include '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple', a 2026 zombie thriller featuring Ralph Fiennes, and classic films like 'Atonement'.
HBO Max and Hulu are streaming Anne Hathaway and Meryl Streep's 2006 fashion comedy 'The Devil Wears Prada'.
Jim Carrey's 'The Truman Show', a 1998 film about unwitting reality TV, is available on Paramount+.

title: Details emerge on fatal police shooting of 27-year-old woman in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Megan Whiting, 27, was shot by police while armed with a knife near an injured resident in Northfield, NH.
Officers responded to a 911 call reporting Whiting attacking a woman with a fire extinguisher and damaging property.
Whiting's cause of death was multiple gunshot wounds; the officer involved remains unidentified pending investigation.
